title: Fixing a Node.js Express Middleware Route for Dynamic Route Resolution

statement.markdown: Implement a Node.js Express middleware route to handle dynamic routes. The route should return a 404 status code if the route does not exist. The parameter should be extracted from the URL path. You are given the following Express app setup:
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a URL path as a string.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the resolved route handler function or a 404 status code if the route does not exist.
statement.notes: The middleware route should handle both static and dynamic routes.

examples.explanation: The route handler for the /hello/:name route should return 'Hello John!' for the input /hello/John.
The route handler for the /hello/:name route should return a 404 status code for the input /hello.
The route handler for the /bye route should return 'Goodbye!' for the input /bye.

hints.text: Check the Express app setup for the required modules.
Use the app.use() method to define the middleware route.

editorial.title: Editorial: Node.js Express Middleware Route for Dynamic Route Resolution
editorial.intuition: The middleware route should handle dynamic routes by extracting the parameter from the URL path.
editorial.bruteForce: Use a switch statement to check the route and return the corresponding handler function.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a regular expression to extract the parameter from the URL path and return the corresponding handler function.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use Express.js params middleware to extract the parameter from the URL path.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The middleware route should return the correct handler function for each input URL path.
editorial.dryRun: Test the middleware route with different input URL paths.
